queries.name: Semantic Content Discovery - Thematic Similarity
queries.pain_point: Keyword-based search misses semantic intent and nuanced preferences - 34% of search results dismissed without engagement
queries.description: Find content with similar thematic elements using semantic understanding beyond surface-level genre matching. Enables users to discover content based on narrative complexity, emotional tone, and thematic resonance.
queries.example_esql:
FROM content_catalog METADATA _score
| WHERE MATCH(description, "complex family dynamics with moral ambiguity and redemption arc")
  AND availability == "on-demand"
| KEEP content_id, _score, title, themes, genre, narrative_complexity
| SORT _score DESC
| LIMIT 15

queries.name: Weighted Hybrid Search - Balancing Semantic and Exact Match
queries.pain_point: No understanding of content attributes beyond surface-level metadata - cannot distinguish narrative complexity, tone, or perspective
queries.description: Advanced relevance tuning that balances semantic understanding (75% weight) with exact keyword matching (25% weight) for optimal content discovery. This best practice approach ensures users find both conceptually similar content and exact title matches.
queries.example_esql:
FROM content_catalog METADATA _score
| WHERE MATCH(description, "psychological thriller mystery", {"boost": 0.75})
   OR MATCH(title, "psychological thriller mystery", {"boost": 0.25})
| KEEP content_id, _score, title, description, tone, narrative_complexity
| SORT _score DESC
| LIMIT 20

queries.name: Fuzzy Search for Typo-Tolerant Content Discovery
queries.pain_point: Keyword-based search misses semantic intent - users abandon searches due to typos or misspellings
queries.description: Handle user typos and misspellings automatically using fuzzy matching. Critical for improving search success rates when users search by actor names, director names, or show titles with uncertain spelling.
queries.example_esql:
FROM content_catalog METADATA _score
| WHERE MATCH(cast, "Bennedict Cumberbatch", {"fuzziness": "AUTO"})
   OR MATCH(director, "Christofer Nolan", {"fuzziness": "AUTO"})
| KEEP content_id, _score, title, cast, director, genre
| SORT _score DESC
| LIMIT 25

queries.name: Preference-Aware Content Retrieval with Negative Filtering
queries.pain_point: Binary recommendation logic ignores negative signals and context - users who skip content repeatedly still receive similar recommendations
queries.description: Retrieve content matching user's preferred themes while actively filtering out disliked themes and avoided genres. Addresses the critical gap where users repeatedly see content they've explicitly rejected.
queries.example_esql:
FROM content_catalog METADATA _score
| WHERE MATCH(description, "space exploration with scientific accuracy")
| LOOKUP JOIN user_preferences ON user_id
| WHERE user_preferences.user_id == "U123456"
  AND themes IN user_preferences.liked_themes
  AND themes NOT IN user_preferences.disliked_themes
  AND genre NOT IN user_preferences.avoided_genres
| KEEP content_id, _score, title, themes, genre, tone
| SORT _score DESC
| LIMIT 10

queries.name: Explainable Recommendation Retrieval
queries.pain_point: No explainability in recommendations - 42% of users report lower trust due to black-box collaborative filtering
queries.description: Find recommendation explanations that transparently show why content was suggested. Enables users to understand the reasoning behind recommendations and provide feedback.
queries.example_esql:
FROM recommendation_explanations METADATA _score
| WHERE user_id == "U123456"
  AND MATCH(explanation_text, "similar themes to shows you enjoyed")
| LOOKUP JOIN content_catalog ON content_id
| KEEP recommendation_id, _score, content_catalog.title, explanation_text, reasoning_factors, similarity_score
| SORT similarity_score DESC
| LIMIT 10

queries.name: Multi-Term Precision Search with Minimum Match Threshold
queries.pain_point: Static content taxonomies from 2015 requiring manual updates - search returns too many irrelevant results
queries.description: Advanced precision control requiring at least 3 out of 5 search terms to match, reducing noise in multi-keyword searches. Best practice for complex user queries with multiple optional criteria.
queries.example_esql:
FROM content_catalog METADATA _score
| WHERE MATCH(description, "female protagonist action adventure strong character development", {
    "operator": "OR",
    "minimum_should_match": 3
  })
| KEEP content_id, _score, title, description, themes, subgenres
| SORT _score DESC
| LIMIT 20

queries.name: Cold Start Content Discovery - Thematic Expansion
queries.pain_point: Cold start problems - takes 20-40 viewing sessions to develop reasonable preference model, 18% new subscriber dissatisfaction
queries.description: For new users with minimal viewing history, discover content by exploring related themes semantically. Helps reduce cold start time from 20-40 sessions to 3-5 sessions by intelligently expanding initial preferences.
queries.example_esql:
FROM content_themes METADATA _score
| WHERE MATCH(theme_description, "coming of age self discovery identity")
| LOOKUP JOIN content_catalog ON theme_id
| WHERE content_catalog.themes == content_themes.theme_name
| KEEP content_catalog.content_id, _score, content_catalog.title, content_themes.theme_name, content_themes.related_themes
| SORT _score DESC
| LIMIT 12
